rationale: In the citing paper, Rindfleisch et al. (2008) is used as broad methodological background, mainly to support claims that triangulation/longitudinal approaches can be costly and difficult and that cross-sectional survey settings are common. The citing paper does not build its core hypotheses, key constructs, or estimation strategy directly from this source. The role of the citation is contextual positioning rather than a distinct conceptual or methodological dependency.
